title: podman/libpod: add default AppArmor profile
body:
Make users of libpod more secure by adding the libpod/apparmor package
to load a pre-defined AppArmor profile.  Large chunks of libpod/apparmor
come from github.com/moby/moby.

Also check if a specified AppArmor profile is actually loaded and throw
an error if necessary.

The default profile is loaded only on Linux builds with the `apparmor`
buildtag enabled.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body:
If this code was copied out of Moby, why not copy out the docker parser code as well?
I think the proper directory for this would be libpod/pkg/apparmor  THen we could use it in CRI-O and Buildah.  Although I don't have a lot of experience with AppArmor.  :^)

> If this code was copied out of Moby, why not copy out the docker parser code as well?

The only remaining part is the wrapper code around `apparmor_parser`. I can move that code into this package as well if you prefer.

> I think the proper directory for this would be libpod/pkg/apparmor THen we could use it in CRI-O and Buildah. Although I don't have a lot of experience with AppArmor. :^)

Sounds good to me.

@rhatdan I'd like to add apparmor to the testing vector, but that's linked to the common voodoo requiring something like that https://github.com/vrothberg/libpod/blob/e089223ef9475f514a9c6a42e98bd06aaa9a7405/hack/dind. Any objections?

I would like to get rid of dependencies on Docker whenever possible, definitely do not want to add more.

Having a github.com/containers/apparmor might not be a bad idea either.  Or in github.com/opencontainers/

> I would like to get rid of dependencies on Docker whenever possible, definitely do not want to add more.

Understood. I'll break out the `aaparser` code then.

> Having a github.com/containers/apparmor might not be a bad idea either. Or in github.com/opencontainers/

I'd prefer to have it in libpod for now as all infrastructure is already in place. Once that's working I'm absolutely okay with with breaking that out into github.com/{open,}containers.
@rhatdan atomic CI is broken. I hope I didn't kill any machine :fearful:
@vrothberg I think our CI's default state is broken. It's just decided to take a vacation today.
I'm getting more and more convinced that Travis has issues with AppArmor, but I'll continue tomorrow with a fresh pair of eyes.

It turns out Travis doesn't enable AppArmor. The "workaround" for that is to make `hack/apparmor_tag.sh` sensitive to the `TRAVIS` env variable, which means that AppArmor support must be tested locally for now. It's better than nothing, but I am not happy about Travis.

Regarding the PR. I consider it ready to go :)
I'm fine with merging now, though we really need to look into improving our CI situation.

> I'm fine with merging now, though we really need to look into improving our CI situation.

I absolutely agree. Any idea what we could do?
